metadata.title: Why the Wrong AI Surface Costs More Than the Wrong Prompt

beats.title: Liam cold open — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Olá, Liam — in for Bear. The data files were never included? Can you explain it, Bear?

props.command: The data files were never included?
props.segment: Why the Wrong AI Surface Costs

beats.narration_text: A policy analyst asked Claude to find patterns in her program data. Claude returned a confident, well-organized analysis. The data files were never included.

production_viz.label: confident analysis — wrong context
production_viz.mechanic: a chat window chip (slate) shows a teal CONFIDENT ANALYSIS output; below it a crimson tag: 'DATA FILES NEVER INCLUDED'

beats.narration_text: The output looked exactly like the output from the right surface. She had no way to tell the difference from reading it.
beats.scene_description: policy analyst at desk looking at a polished report printout with a furrowed brow, laptop visible; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment; slow push-in
beats.image_prompt: B02, policy analyst at desk studying a printed report with a furrowed brow, laptop open behind, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible, collage aesthetic
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] analyst studying AI output she cannot verify

beats.narration_text: A policy analyst asked Claude to find patterns in her program data. Claude returned a confident, well-organized analysis. The data files were never included. Why didn't the output reveal this?

card.copy: Why didn't the output reveal the context was missing?

beats.narration_text: You might expect that if Claude lacks the context it needs, it would say so — or produce noticeably weaker output. A good tool should know when it does not have what it needs.

production_viz.label: naive model: missing context produces weak output
production_viz.mechanic: chip 'MISSING CONTEXT' (crimson) -> arrow -> chip 'WEAK OUTPUT' (crimson); labeled 'the naive expectation — it would be obvious'

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] the naive model — missing context should be visible

beats.narration_text: Claude generates fluent, well-organized text from whatever is in its context window. If the context window contains pasted notes instead of the actual files, it produces fluent analysis of the pasted notes. The output quality is indistinguishable from analysis based on the full dataset.

production_viz.label: context window determines what Claude can see
production_viz.mechanic: two context window boxes side by side: left contains PASTED NOTES chip (crimson) -> produces CONFIDENT ANALYSIS (looks teal); right contains FULL FILES chip (teal) -> produces CONFIDENT ANALYSIS (teal); outputs look identical

beats.narration_text: The three Claude surfaces are designed for different context sources. Chat for conversational context — what you paste or type. Code for repository context — the whole codebase. Cowork for file context — folders, connected apps, calendars. Each surface can only see the context it is designed to access.

production_viz.label: three surfaces — three context sources
production_viz.mechanic: three chips in a column: 'CLAUDE CHAT' (slate) labeled 'context: pasted / typed'; 'CLAUDE CODE' (slate) labeled 'context: code repository'; 'CLAUDE COWORK' (slate) labeled 'context: files / apps / calendar'; each has a different key icon

beats.narration_text: A task with context in the wrong place for the surface you chose will fail — not because Claude is incompetent, but because it cannot see what it needs. The output will look the same either way.

card.copy: The surface cannot see the context it did not receive.

beats.narration_text: Alex opens chat, pastes one meeting note, gets a polished paragraph. Sam opens Cowork, points it at the notes folder, and gets a synthesis with source citations. Both outputs look equally professional. Only one is based on the actual data.

production_viz.label: Alex vs Sam: same task, different surfaces
production_viz.mechanic: two columns: ALEX uses CHAT (crimson) — one note pasted -> polished paragraph (looks teal but crimson tag: 'based on 1 of 12 notes'); SAM uses COWORK (teal) — folder pointed -> synthesis with citations (teal)

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] Alex and Sam — same task, different surfaces

beats.narration_text: The five routing questions — output type, context source, risk, reversibility, and verification path — take less than a minute to run before choosing a surface. The analyst skipped them. She chose the surface closest at hand.

production_viz.label: five routing questions before surface choice
production_viz.mechanic: five chips in a vertical list: 'OUTPUT TYPE', 'CONTEXT SOURCE', 'RISK', 'REVERSIBILITY', 'VERIFICATION PATH' — all teal; each question resolves to a surface recommendation; labeled 'takes less than a minute'

beats.narration_text: The practice move: before any Claude task, ask where the necessary context lives. In your clipboard — chat. In a code repository — Code. In a folder of files or connected apps — Cowork. The context source determines the surface.

production_viz.label: context source -> surface routing decision
production_viz.mechanic: three routing paths: 'CLIPBOARD / TYPED' -> CHAT (teal); 'CODE REPO' -> CODE (teal); 'FILES / APPS' -> COWORK (teal); a branching decision tree

beats.new_visual_element: [GRAPHIC] routing decision — context source determines surface

beats.narration_text: The check: before any Claude task, ask — where is the necessary context? Is the surface I chose designed to access that context? If not, stop. Choose the right surface first.

production_viz.label: the routing check — does surface match context?
production_viz.mechanic: a decision diamond: 'Does this surface access the required context?' -> YES (teal) -> 'PROCEED'; NO (crimson) -> 'CHOOSE RIGHT SURFACE FIRST'; the YES path is highlighted

beats.narration_text: Capability is not fit. Claude will accept whatever task you give it on whatever surface you give it on. It will not tell you the surface cannot see what it needs. The routing decision is yours.

card.copy: Capability is not fit. The routing decision is yours.

beats.narration_text: For the analyst: point Cowork at the notes folder — not chat. The source log tells you which files contributed to each finding. The verification path exists because the surface can see the context.
beats.scene_description: policy analyst at desk organizing a folder of documents before pointing software at them, deliberate and methodical; editorial newsprint desaturated treatment; slow push-in
beats.image_prompt: B13, policy analyst at desk organizing a stack of documents and folders before starting a task, deliberate methodical expression, editorial desaturated newsprint treatment, flat print lighting, no text legible, collage aesthetic
beats.new_visual_element: [PHOTO] analyst preparing context before choosing surface

beats.narration_text: The wrong AI surface costs more than the wrong prompt — because the prompt can be revised, but if the surface cannot see the context, no prompt improvement helps. Ask where the context lives before you type.

card.copy: No prompt fixes a surface that cannot see the context.

beats.narration_text: The routing decision is not about Claude. It is about context access. Choose the surface that can see what your task requires — or the output will be fluent and wrong.

card.copy: Choose the surface that can see what the task requires.

beats.title: Liam verdict — ClaudeVerdictArtifact
beats.narration_text: Here's what the body just demonstrated. The wrong AI surface costs more than the wrong prompt — because the prompt can be revised, but if the surface cannot see the context, no prompt improvement helps. Ask where the context lives before you type. The routing decision is not about Claude. It is about context access. Choose the surface that can see what your task requires — or the output will be fluent and wrong.

beats.title: Your turn handoff — ClaudeComposerAsk
beats.narration_text: Take this prompt, run it on your own — pick any cancer type or clinical scenario you know about and ask how this mechanism applies there.

props.command: Explain how [Why the Wrong AI Surface Costs More Than the Wrong Prompt] applies to a specific cancer type or clinical case you're studying. What proteins are involved, what goes wrong at the molecular level, and what is the therapeutic or diagnostic significance?
